title: Avoid excessively long source files
description: When source files become too large, they tend to accumulate multiple responsibilities and become difficult to understand and maintain. Large files are harder to navigate, test, and debug, leading to reduced code quality.
recommendation: Refactor large files into smaller, focused files with well-defined responsibilities.

noncompliant.description: A single file trying to handle too many responsibilities, making it difficult to maintain and understand.

compliant.description: Code organized into focused files, each with a single responsibility and manageable size.
